Send your CV to:</p><p>rishita.solanki@merillife.com</p> exec, marketing, sales Madhya Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, India 2026-06-10T16:26:08+00:00 1133165 https://remoteOK.com/remote-jobs/remote-district-associate-manager-meril-1133165 https://remoteOK.com/assets/logo-square.png Lawyer Queensland Government Intellectually stimulating, professionally engaging, personally rewarding and lifestyle accommodating is the basis of a career with Crown Law. With five legal branches and 15 teams, a broad range of legal services are offered with many opportunities.<br><br>You will enjoy a flexible working environment with excellent employment conditions.<br><br>We offer competitive salary packages and generous superannuation. Other benefits include part-time arrangements, flexible working hours, various leave provisions, telecommuting, reward and recognition programs and employee assistance schemes.<br><br>Crown Law is committed to the personal and professional development of all staff, providing a number of programs to help staff reach their full potential. Our values are - Integrity, Excellence, Respect and Responsibility.<br><br><strong>The legal work handled within the Constitutional Law Team includes:</strong> <br><br><ul><li> Providing advice on complex questions of Commonwealth and state constitutional law, including advice about:</li><ul><li>notices issued under s.78B of the Judiciary Act 1903 (Cth)</li><li>inconsistency of Commonwealth and State laws</li><li>limits on State power including issues relating to the application of Chapter III of the Constitution, and the implied freedom of communication about government and political matters</li></ul><li> Advising on complex and significant matters to government, including:</li><ul><li>statutory interpretation in relation to matters that do not fall within any other team's specific area of expertise</li><li>miscellaneous Government law matters in very complex matters of fundamental importance to the State</li><li>advising the Attorney-General in relation to their role as the First Law Officer, including ministerial correspondence and grants of fiat.</li></ul><li> Providing advice, and potentially representation in, matters involving the interpretation and application of the Human Rights Act 2019. This may include:</li><ul><li>providing advice to Crown Law's clients about the proper interpretation of legislation, and the compatibility of proposed decisions with human rights</li><li>providing advice to the Attorney-General about notices issued under ss 52 and 53 of the Human Rights Act</li><li>assisting clients with the preparation of Statements of Compatibility for legislation</li></ul><li> Instructing the Solicitor-General and other counsel in complex matters about these areas in the High Court and other Courts (State and Federal). <br><br></li></ul><strong>Applications to remain current for 12 months.<br><br></strong><strong>Job Ad Reference: QLD/691390/26<br><br></strong><strong>Closing Date: Wednesday, 24 th June 2026<br><br></strong><strong>Occupational group</strong> Justice &amp; Legal legal, telecommuting, part time Brisbane, 2026-06-10T15:40:11+00:00 1133195 https://remoteOK.com/remote-jobs/remote-lawyer-queensland-government-1133195 https://remoteOK.com/assets/logo-square.png Clinical Coding Trainer Torbay and South Devon NHS Foundation Trust An exciting opportunity has arisen for an NHS England Approved Clinical Coding Trainer to join the Torbay and South Devon (TSD) Clinical Coding Team.<br><br>We are seeking a skilled and motivated trainer to deliver a comprehensive training programme for Clinical Coding trainees and apprentices, while also supporting the ongoing development of our wider coding workforce. You will play a key role in promoting highâquality clinical coding across the Trust and supporting colleagues to deepen their understanding of coding standards and processes.<br><br>The ideal candidate will bring substantial NHS experience, excellent communication skills, and strong leadership qualities. We offer flexible working arrangements, including hybrid and remote options, alongside a competitive benefits package.<br><br>Applicants must be NHS England Approved Clinical Coding Trainers. We will also consider applicants who have secured a place on the NHS England 2026/27 Clinical Coding Training Programme (CCTP) Assessment Day (26 June).<br><br><strong>Important</strong>: 15% Recruitment &amp; Retention Premium included<br><br>The main purpose of the post is to work with the Clinical Coding Management team to<br><br><ul><li>Develop, plan and deliver training courses, workshops and adâhoc sessions for clinical coders and related staff.</li><li>Conduct induction for new staff, advise the Coding Manager on ongoing training and development needs, and act as mentor for novice coders.</li><li>Assess learning needs and shape trainee pathways.</li><li>Implement and deliver regular training sessions for clinical coders.</li><li>Develop and update training materials, ensure adherence to national coding standards, and support the production of case study training content.</li><li>Provide coaching, conduct feedback sessions and resolve coding inaccuracies in a professional manner.</li><li>Act as a point of contact for the Clinical Coding Department and contribute to policy and procedural development.<br><br></li></ul>Here at Torbay and South Devon NHS Foundation Trust patients are at the heart of everything we do. We are a pioneering, integrated Care organisation -The first in in England to combine acute hospital, community and Social Care serving 286,000 residents.
